base_prompt: Calculate the ratio of N days (default 5) mean of close price divided by its previous close price to N days (default 5) standard deviation of close price divided by its previous close price, multiplied by the ratio of N days (default 10) mean volume to N days (default 10) standard deviation of volume.

Find the product of the N days (default 5) mean of close divided by prior close over N days (default 5) std of that ratio, and N days (default 10) mean volume over its N days (default 10) std.

Derive the factor as (N days (default 5) mean of close / previous close divided by N days (default 5) std of close / previous close) times (N days (default 10) mean volume over N days (default 10) std volume).
Calculate (N days (default 5) mean of close price over previous close / N days (default 5) std of that) * (N days (default 10) mean of volume / N days (default 10) std of volume).

Obtain (N days (default 5) mean of close / previous close divided by N days (default 5) std of close / previous close) multiplied by (N days (default 10) mean volume / N days (default 10) std volume).
Formulate factor: (mean of close / REF close N days (default 5) / std of same) * (mean of volume N days (default 10) / std of volume N days (default 10)).
Get (N days (default 5) mean of close over prior close / N days (default 5) std of close over prior close) * (N days (default 10) mean of volume / N days (default 10) std of volume).
Create factor: multiply N days (default 5) mean of close over REF close / N days (default 5) std of same with N days (default 10) mean of volume over N days (default 10) std volume.

base_prompt: Calculate the difference between the N-day (default 5) mean of (close price times volume) and the N-day (default 20) mean of (close price times volume), divided by the N-day (default 20) mean of the close price.
synonym_prompts: Compute the N-day (default 5) average of (close times volume) less the N-day (default 20) average of (close times volume), all over the N-day (default 20) average close.
Find the result of (N-day (default 5) mean (close * volume) minus N-day (default 20) mean (close * volume)) divided by the N-day (default 20) mean close.
Derive the factor as (N-day (default 5) mean of (close times volume) minus N-day (default 20) mean of (close times volume)) divided by (N-day (default 20) mean of close).
Calculate (N-day (default 5) mean of (close * volume) - N-day (default 20) mean of (close * volume)) / (N-day (default 20) mean of close).

Generate a factor by dividing the difference between the N-day (default 5) mean of (close times volume) and the N-day (default 20) mean of (close times volume) by the N-day (default 20) mean of close.
Obtain (N-day (default 5) mean of (close times volume) minus N-day (default 20) mean of (close times volume)) divided by (N-day (default 20) mean of close).
Formulate factor: (mean of (close * volume) N days (default 5) - mean of (close * volume) N days (default 20)) / mean of close N days (default 20).
Get (N-day (default 5) mean of (close times volume) less N-day (default 20) mean of (close times volume)) over N-day (default 20) mean of close.
Create factor: divide (N-day (default 5) mean of (close * volume) - N-day (default 20) mean of (close * volume)) by N-day (default 20) mean of close.

base_prompt: Calculate a factor that is 1 if the close is greater than the previous day's N-day (default 20) maximum high, and 0 otherwise, multiplied by the ratio of volume to the N-day (default 20) mean of volume.
synonym_prompts: Compute a factor: 1 if close exceeds prior day's N-day (default 20) high, else 0, times volume divided by N-day (default 20) average volume.
Find the product of (1 if close > prior N-day (default 20) max high, else 0) and (volume / N-day (default 20) mean volume).
Derive the factor as (if close is greater than the previous day's N-day (default 20) maximum high, then 1, else 0) times (volume divided by N-day (default 20) mean volume).
Calculate (close > Ref(Max(high, 20), 1)) * (volume / Mean(volume, 20)).
Generate a factor by multiplying the boolean result of (close > prior day's N-day (default 20) max high) with (volume / N-day (default 20) mean volume).
Obtain (1 if close is above the previous day's N-day (default 20) maximum high, otherwise 0) multiplied by (volume divided by N-day (default 20) mean volume).
Formulate factor: (close GT REF(MAX(high, N days (default 20)), 1)) * (volume / mean of volume N days (default 20)).
Get (close greater than prior day's N-day (default 20) max high) times (volume over N-day (default 20) mean volume).
Create factor: multiply (close > Ref(Max(high, 20), 1)) with (volume / Mean(volume, 20)).

base_prompt: Multiply a boolean indicating whether the close is greater than the previous day's high by the ratio of the current volume to the N-day (default 20) mean volume.
synonym_prompts: Compute (close greater than prior high) times (volume divided by N-day (default 20) average volume).

Calculate (close > Ref($high, 1)) * (volume / Mean($volume, 20)).
Generate a factor by multiplying the boolean result of close exceeding prior high by the volume relative to its N-day (default 20) average.

Formulate factor: (close > previous high) * (volume / mean of volume N days (default 20)).
Get (close exceeds prior day's high) times (volume over N-day (default 20) mean volume).
Create factor: multiply (close > prior high) with (volume / N-day (default 20) mean volume).

base_prompt: Generate a signal that is 1 if the close is greater than the previous day's high, AND the volume is greater than twice the N-day (default 20) mean of volume, otherwise 0.
synonym_prompts: Check if close exceeds prior high AND volume is more than double the N-day (default 20) average volume; if so, return 1, else 0.
Is the close above yesterday's high AND volume greater than 2 times the N-day (default 20) average volume? Return 1 if true, 0 otherwise.
Create a factor that equals 1 when close > previous high AND volume > 2 * N-day (default 20) mean volume; else 0.
If close is higher than the previous high AND volume is greater than twice the N-day (default 20) average volume, then the factor is 1, otherwise it is 0.

If close breaches the previous high AND volume surpasses twice the N-day (default 20) average volume, return 1, else return 0.

base_prompt: Calculate the absolute value of (close minus open) divided by the N-day (default 5) range (max high minus min low), multiplied by the N-day (default 10) mean of volume.
synonym_prompts: Compute the absolute difference between close and open, over the N-day (default 5) range, times the N-day (default 10) average volume.

Derive the factor as (absolute value of (close minus open) divided by (N-day (default 5) max high minus N-day (default 5) min low)) times (N-day (default 10) mean of volume).

Formulate factor: (abs(close - open) / (max high - min low N days (default 5))) * (mean of volume N days (default 10)).

Create factor: multiply abs(close - open) / (N-day (default 5) max high - N-day (default 5) min low) with N-day (default 10) mean of volume.

base_prompt: Calculate the N-day (default 10) mean of the close price minus the close price N days (default 20) ago, divided by the current close price, multiplied by a boolean indicating whether the N-day (default 5) mean of volume is greater than the same mean N days (default 10) ago.

synonym_prompts: Compute the N-day (default 20) ranking of (N-day (default 5) average (close over prior close) / N-day (default 10) close std) times (volume square root plus close-volume N-day (default 5) correlation).

Calculate the N-day (default 20) rank of ((N-day (default 5) mean(close / Ref(close, 1)) / N-day (default 10) Std(close)) * (Sqrt(volume) + N-day (default 5) Correlation(close, volume))).

base_prompt: Calculate a factor that combines price momentum, volume surge, and correlation ranking. It is 1 if the percentage change in close price over the past 5 days exceeds 2%, AND the 3-day average volume is greater than 1.5 times the 20-day average volume. The result is then multiplied by the N-day (default 20) rank of the correlation between close price and volume over the past N days (default 10).
synonym_prompts: Compute a factor: (close price increased by more than 2% over the last 5 days) AND (recent 3-day volume exceeds 1.5x the 20-day average volume), scaled by the N-day (default 20) rank of the correlation between price and volume.
Find a signal where (5-day price change > 2%) AND (3-day average volume > 1.5 * 20-day average volume), weighted by the N-day (default 20) rank of price-volume correlation.
Derive a factor that is the product of (price up > 2% in 5 days), (volume surge: 3-day avg > 1.5x 20-day avg), and the N-day (default 20) ranked price-volume correlation.
Calculate ((($close - Ref($close, 5)) / Ref($close, 5)) > 0.02) * (Mean($volume, 3) > Mean($volume, 20) * 1.5) * Rank(Correlation($close, $volume, 10), 20).
Generate a factor by combining a price momentum condition (5-day change > 2%), a volume surge condition (3-day avg > 1.5x 20-day avg), and the N-day (default 20) ranking of price-volume correlation.
Obtain a factor that is the result of (price momentum > 2%) AND (volume surge > 1.5x), modulated by the N-day (default 20) rank of price-volume correlation.
Formulate factor: (price momentum > 2%) * (volume surge > 1.5x) * N-day (default 20) rank of (price-volume correlation).
Get (price up more than 2% in 5 days) AND (volume surge: 3-day avg > 1.5x 20-day avg), all scaled by N-day (default 20) ranked price-volume correlation.
Create factor: multiply (price momentum > 2%) with (volume surge > 1.5x), and then multiply by the N-day (default 20) rank of price-volume correlation.

factor_gt: Rank((Delta($close, 10) / Ref($close, 10)) * Corr($close, $volume, 5), 20) * If(Mean($volume, 5) > Mean($volume, 20), 1, 0) * ($pe_ttm < Med($pe_ttm, 20))
base_prompt: Calculate a factor that combines price momentum, price-volume correlation, a volume breakout filter, and a value screen. It ranks the product of the 10-day percentage price change and the 5-day price-volume correlation over N days (default 20). This is then multiplied by a boolean (1 or 0) indicating whether the 5-day average volume is greater than the 20-day average volume. Finally, this is multiplied by a boolean (1 or 0) indicating whether the price-to-earnings ratio is less than the median price-to-earnings ratio over the past N days (default 20).
synonym_prompts: Compute a score based on (momentum * correlation), ranked over N days (default 20), filtered by (short-term volume exceeding long-term volume), and screened by (PE ratio below median).
Find a factor that combines (momentum strength), (price-volume relationship), (volume breakout confirmation), and (value investing) principles.
Derive a factor that is the product of (ranked momentum * correlation), (volume breakout), and (value screen).
Calculate Rank((Delta(close, 10) / Ref(close, 10)) * Corr(close, volume, 5), 20) * If(Mean(volume, 5) > Mean(volume, 20), 1, 0) * (pe_ttm < Med(pe_ttm, 20)).
Generate a factor that integrates ranked momentum-correlation, volume confirmation, and a PE ratio-based value screen.
Obtain a signal that is the combination of ranked momentum-correlation, a volume surge indicator, and a value screen based on PE ratio.
Formulate factor: Rank(momentum * correlation) * (volume breakout) * (value screen).
Get a factor that is the product of ranked momentum-correlation, a volume breakout signal, and a value screen based on PE ratio.
Create factor: multiply (ranked momentum * correlation) with (volume breakout), and then multiply by (value screen).

base_prompt: Calculate a factor that combines volatility, momentum, and a price-volume correlation filter. It ranks the ratio of the N-day (default 10) standard deviation of (high minus low) to the N-day (default 20) mean of close price over N days (default 50). This is multiplied by the N-day (default 5) percentage price change. Finally, this is multiplied by a boolean (1 or 0) indicating whether the N-day (default 5) price-volume correlation is greater than 0.2.
synonym_prompts: Compute a score based on (volatility measure), (momentum), and a (price-volume correlation filter).
Find a factor that combines (volatility), (momentum), and (correlation) principles.

Formulate factor: Rank(volatility measure) * (momentum) * (correlation filter).

base_prompt: Calculate a factor that combines volatility, momentum, and a volume confirmation. It ranks the ratio of the N-day (default 10) standard deviation of the close price to the N-day (default 10) mean of the close price over N days (default 20). This is then multiplied by the N-day (default 5) percentage change in the close price. Finally, this is multiplied by a boolean (1 or 0) indicating whether the current volume is greater than the previous day's N-day (default 20) average volume.
